evidence: Implemented builder validation + API guard for capability ceiling and policy coherence: (1) Added real-time client-side validation in assistant-manager.tsx that computes ceiling violations as user edits policy rules, showing inline amber errors on each rule row ('Tick the {Family} box in Declared capabilities to allow this rule to take effect'); (2) Highlighted violated capability checkboxes with amber ring/bg; (3) Added violation summary text at top of capabilities section; (4) Blocked form submission when violations exist (scrolls to capabilities section); (5) Disabled submit button with tooltip; (6) API-level guard already existed in CreateAssistant/UpdateAssistant via ValidatePolicyAgainstCeiling — confirmed still working. TypeScript compiles clean. All 31 Go packages pass.
Inline validation, checkbox highlighting, submission blocking, and disabled submit button all implemented in assistant-manager.tsx as part of the ceiling validation feature.

Driver decision: reuse felixgeelhaar/scout (which wraps Playwright internally) rather than embed chromedp/playwright-go directly. Scout already solves browser lifecycle, isolated profiles, semantic observation, network capture, and accessibility — duplicating in nomid would be ceremony with no benefit. Trade-off accepted: external scout binary dependency, installable via `brew install scout`. Implementation in internal/plugins/browser/mcp.go (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio MCP client: subprocess management, initialize handshake, concurrent CallTool with response routing via per-id channels, 16 MiB read buffer for screenshot bytes, graceful 3s shutdown then SIGKILL). Sandbox model: one Scout subprocess per Connection so each assistant gets an isolated browser profile.

15 tools wired in internal/plugins/browser/plugin.go + tool.go: navigate, observe, click, type, fill_form (semantic), extract, extract_table, screenshot, readable_text, markdown, wait_for, has_element, scroll_by, dismiss_cookies, console_errors. Each Nomi tool maps to one Scout MCP tool; passArgs() generates the per-tool arg projection. Lazy per-Connection client spawn so Stop tears down idle clients with the others. decodeMCPResult unwraps the standard MCP `{content: [{type, text}]}` envelope and tries JSON-then-plain-text fallback so structured Scout responses surface as map[string]interface{} and plain text gets a `{text: ...}` wrapper. 14 unit tests against a stub MCP invoker covering manifest, dispatch (navigate/type/fill_form/observe/screenshot/extract), error surfacing, binding rejection, disabled connection, Start/Stop idempotency, decodeMCPResult shape coverage. Whole 31-package suite green.

Per-domain gating shipped via per-Connection allowed_hosts allowlist (internal/plugins/browser/gate.go). Wildcard matching mirrors the existing wasmhost matcher (exact, *.suffix with leading-dot anchor that rejects "slack.com.attacker.com" impersonation, and explicit "*" escape hatch). Empty allowed_hosts denies all navigation — never silently allowed. browser.navigate is the gate point; subsequent click/type/extract/etc. trust the loaded page (matches user mental model: "I told it to use example.com; clicking buttons there is normal"). Connection config field added to manifest with required=true and a clear description. Tool dispatcher (tool.go) checks gate before clientFor so a deny path never even spawns Scout for the call. Crucial test: TestNavigate_DeniedByConnectionAllowlist asserts the MCP invoker has zero calls when the gate fires (a leaky gate would defeat the security purpose). 11 gate tests + existing 14 plugin tests, all green. Whole 31-package suite green.

DEFERRED to a separate follow-up: (a) session/tab scoping by (assistant_id, session_id) using Scout's tab APIs, (b) idle-timeout cleanup of per-session tabs, (c) integration with the runtime approval flow for "confirm" mode (today the gate is binary allow/deny based on the connection allowlist; runtime-level approval gating per URL would route through ApprovalManager.RequestApproval but that requires plumbing the manager into the plugin and changing tool execution to be approval-aware, which is a larger refactor that warrants its own task). The current allowlist model is the security-meaningful piece; the approval flow is the UX-richer piece.

internal/plugins/calendar/{provider,google,plugin,plugin_test}.go: Provider interface abstracts the calendar backend (ListUpcoming, CreateEvent, UpdateEvent, DeleteEvent, FindFreeSlots). GoogleProvider calls Calendar v3 REST directly (no heavy SDK dep) via the shared integrations/google.OAuthManager. providerFor(conn) routes on conn.Config.provider ("google" v1; "outlook" returns a clear not-implemented error). FindFreeSlots merges busy ranges across multiple calendars and inverts against the requested window. Manifest declares 5 tools (list_upcoming/create_event/update_event/delete_event/find_free_slots) with calendar.read + calendar.write + network.outgoing capabilities. 4 unit tests cover manifest shape, int/time coercion, multi-shape attendee slice coercion. Registered in main.go alongside the other plugins. Full backend sweep across 18 packages green.

Tools landed in the same calendar-01 commit: calendar.list_upcoming, calendar.create_event, calendar.update_event, calendar.delete_event, calendar.find_free_slots. Each enforces the role=tool binding hard wall via plugins.ConnectionNotBoundError, resolves the provider through providerFor(conn), and normalizes Google Calendar wire shape to the plugin's Event struct. RFC3339 time parsing with sensible defaults (from=now, to=from+7d, duration=30min); limit defaults to 25. deleteEvent is idempotent — 404 from Google is treated as success.

internal/plugins/calendar/trigger.go — three trigger kinds (calendar.pre_meeting, calendar.event_created, calendar.event_cancelled) backed by Provider.ListUpcoming polling. Push notifications deferred (Google Calendar's watch API needs an externally-reachable webhook URL — out of scope for v1's local-first install). One Trigger per (connection × rule). Per-rule state in memory: seenEventIDs (snapshot from previous poll, drives created/cancelled deltas), firedPreMeeting (dedupe set so a 5-min lead with 60s polls doesn't fire 5 times). First poll baseline suppresses event_created firings (existing 24h calendar would otherwise retro-fire on daemon restart); pre_meeting still fires on baseline so a meeting starting in 4 minutes doesn't have to wait an extra interval. event_cancelled excludes events that simply rolled past their start time. SetProviderOverride seam added to the plugin so triggers + tools can be tested against a stub. Manifest gained 3 TriggerContributions with example rule shapes. 12 trigger tests covering manifest, Triggers() materialization (one per rule, malformed/unknown/missing-lead rejection), pollOnce semantics (baseline suppression, event_created on second poll, event_cancelled, elapsed-event exclusion, pre_meeting window match, far-future no-fire, dedupe within window), Start/Stop idempotency, rule parsing, poll interval override. Whole 30-package suite green.

Migration 000012_conversations (up+down) adds plugin_conversations with (plugin_id, connection_id, external_conversation_id) unique index, ON DELETE CASCADE from connection → conversation, ON DELETE SET NULL from conversation → run. Adds nullable runs.conversation_id column. domain.Conversation defined; Run gains ConversationID *string. RunRepository Create/GetByID/Update/scanRuns all thread conversation_id through. ConversationRepository provides FindOrCreate (idempotent by natural key), Create/GetByID/FindByExternal, ListByAssistant/ListByConnection (ordered by updated_at DESC), Touch for activity timestamping, Delete. 3 tests: FindOrCreate idempotency, ListByAssistant ordering after Touch, Run round-trip with conversation_id. Full test suite green + bridge write in api/connectors.go for legacy UI dual-writes into plugin_connections so new Telegram users are unblocked.

Runtime.CreateRunInConversation threads conversation_id through run creation. Telegram plugin's handleMessage now finds-or-creates a Conversation per (bot, chat_id) pair and routes the Run through it; the UI will see multi-message exchanges as one thread. REST endpoints added in internal/api/conversations.go: GET /conversations?assistant_id=… or ?connection_id=…, GET /conversations/:id, DELETE /conversations/:id (linked runs survive with conversation_id set to NULL). Plugin's Conversation repo is optional at construction — passing nil falls back to legacy per-run behavior. Full test suite green across 13 packages.

app/src/types/api.ts: added optional source + conversation_id on Run and a Conversation interface. app/src/lib/api.ts: conversationsApi helper (listByAssistant / listByConnection / get / delete). Chat sidebar renders a source badge next to each run title so users can see which channel (telegram, desktop, etc.) initiated the run — paired with the conversation_id field the backend now emits, the frontend is ready for the richer thread-grouping view that a follow-up polish pass can deliver. Frontend lint clean, tsc clean, 20/20 vitest green, vite production build clean. Backend test sweep also remains green.

internal/plugins/discord/plugin.go implements plugins.Plugin + ChannelProvider + ConnectionHealthReporter via github.com/bwmarrin/discordgo. Per-connection session keyed by bot_token (stashed in secrets.Store). sess.AddHandler(MessageCreate) drops self-messages + other bots, enforces identity allowlist, threads via channel_id, and creates a Run. Channel.Send uses ChannelMessageSend so DMs and guild channel replies both work. Manifest declares "discord" channel kind, required bot_token credential, and Gateway-WebSocket-appropriate intents (GuildMessages + DirectMessages + MessageContent). Registered in main.go. 1 manifest-shape test. Full backend sweep across 17 packages green.

Channel role shipped in the same discord/plugin.go commit. DMs + guild channel messages create Runs; Conversation find-or-create threaded by ChannelID so DMs and guild channels don't collide. Channel.Send posts via ChannelMessageSend. Slash commands + rich interactions deferred to discord-03 (tool role) and a future polish pass. Backend sweep green.

internal/plugins/discord/tools.go: Plugin.Tools() contributes discord.post_message. postMessageTool validates connection_id + channel_id + text, enforces the role=tool binding hard wall via plugins.ConnectionNotBoundError, looks up the cached discordgo session for the connection, and posts via ChannelMessageSend. Manifest updated with the ToolContribution. discord.react / discord.manage_roles deferred. Full backend sweep green across 17 packages; the Email/Slack/Discord tool triad is now symmetric and the permission engine treats all three identically.

internal/plugins/email/transport/transport.go: Config shape (IMAP/SMTP host+port+username+password+from+auth_mech); SendEmail builds RFC 5322 message with Message-ID + In-Reply-To + References headers for threading, handles STARTTLS (port 587) via net/smtp and implicit-TLS (port 465) via its own dialer; FetchNew opens an IMAP TLS session with github.com/emersion/go-imap/v2, logs in, selects INBOX, fetches UIDs > sinceUID returning parsed Message structs (UID, MessageID, InReplyTo, References, From, To, Subject, Body, ReceivedAt) and the new watermark. extractPlainBody strips headers from raw message bytes (handles both CRLF and LF-only). ParseAddress normalizes to bare local@domain for identity-allowlist matching. Deps added: emersion/go-imap/v2, emersion/go-sasl (via tidy). 6 unit tests cover address parsing, header-stripping in both line-ending styles, and the config-validation paths. IDLE, multipart MIME, OAuth XOAUTH2 deferred to follow-up tasks (email-02/03/05). Full backend test sweep across 14 packages green.

internal/plugins/email/plugin.go implements plugins.Plugin + ChannelProvider + ConnectionHealthReporter. Poll loop per enabled connection runs every config.poll_interval_seconds (default 60s) via transport.FetchNew; maintains a per-connection UID watermark so only new messages cross the boundary. handleMessage resolves channel-role assistant via bindings, enforces identity allowlist (with first-contact policy — drop / reply_request_access / queue_approval), derives a thread key from References → InReplyTo → Message-ID and uses FindOrCreate on ConversationRepository so multi-email exchanges thread into one Conversation. goal string concatenates Subject + From + Body for readable display. Channel.Send uses In-Reply-To + References to preserve threading across outbound replies. Manifest declares "email" channel kind, network.outgoing + filesystem.read capabilities, and full ConfigSchema (imap_host, imap_port, smtp_host, smtp_port, username, poll_interval_seconds, first_contact_policy). Registered in main.go alongside Telegram. 4 plugin-level unit tests (manifest shape, recipient extraction, int-from-config variants, policy default) + 6 transport tests. Tool and Trigger roles deferred to email-03/email-04. Full backend test sweep across 15 packages green.

internal/plugins/email/tools.go: Plugin.Tools() contributes email.send. sendTool implements tools.Tool with the network.outgoing capability. Execute validates connection_id, to (single string or []), subject, body, optional in_reply_to + references for threaded replies. Enforces the assistant_connection_bindings hard wall at tool-call time: if the caller's __assistant_id has no tool-role binding to the target connection, returns plugins.ConnectionNotBoundError (wrapped ErrConnectionNotBound). Loads the connection via ConnectionRepository, derives transport.Config (IMAP/SMTP hosts, secret-resolved password), calls transport.SendEmail. Manifest updated to declare the email.send ToolContribution with RequiresConnection=true. 6 unit tests cover required-field validation, capability/name, the multi-shape coerceStringSlice coercion, and the ConnectionNotBoundError sentinel wrap. Search/read tools deferred to a follow-up as they require IMAP SEARCH + per-message BODYSTRUCTURE parsing. Full backend sweep across 17 packages green.

internal/plugins/email/triggers.go: per-connection TriggerRule list (from_contains + subject_contains + body_contains + assistant_id + enabled) stored on conn.Config.trigger_rules. Rules are case-insensitive substring matchers; empty filters are skipped. firstMatchingRule gives predictable first-match-wins priority so users put specific rules before catch-alls. Wired into handleMessage: trigger rules short-circuit the channel-role flow — matched rule fires a Run against the rule's target assistant (threaded into the same Conversation so the user sees one coherent thread). Identity allowlist still gates the trigger path. Manifest declares the inbox_watch TriggerContribution + email.rule_matched event type. Extracted resolveThreadKey helper so channel and trigger paths share the same Message-ID → thread-root logic. 7 unit tests cover filter case-insensitivity, disabled/missing-assistant guards, catch-all semantics, first-match priority, malformed config tolerance, and thread-key resolution preference order. Full backend sweep across 17 packages green; Email plugin now contributes all three roles (channel + tool + trigger).

app/src/lib/email-presets.ts: 7 provider presets (Gmail, Outlook, Fastmail, Yahoo, iCloud, ProtonMail Bridge, generic) with IMAP/SMTP host + port, an authNote explaining the app-password requirement, a docURL linking to setup instructions, and domainMatchers for email-domain auto-detection. detectProviderFromEmail(email) returns the matching preset or generic fallback. app/src/components/plugins-manager.tsx: AddConnectionDialog gains a "Email provider" dropdown (email-plugin only) that pre-fills the host/port config fields and shows the auth note + doc link; typing an email address into the username field auto-picks the provider when it's recognized. General polish: dialog now renders ConfigSchema fields for EVERY plugin (not just credentials), so generic plugins with typed config fields get real inputs instead of no UI; number fields are coerced to JS numbers on submit to match the Go config shape. Frontend tsc/lint/20-vitest/vite-build clean.

Implemented cross-cutting error message improvements: (1) Created structured UserError type with Code/Title/Message/Action fields in internal/domain/user_error.go; (2) Rewrote runtime execution errors (ceiling violation, policy deny, approval denied/remembered, rate limit) to be plain-language and actionable; (3) Rewrote core tool errors (LLM chat, filesystem read/write, command exec, sandbox) with user-friendly messages; (4) Created API respondError helper that detects UserError and serializes structured fields; (5) Updated runs.go to use respondError for all runtime endpoints. All 31 Go packages pass, TypeScript compiles clean.
Completed as part of error-message-audit: runtime execution errors rewritten with plain-language + actionable steps + structured codes.

Completed as part of error-message-audit: API respondError helper serializes structured UserError fields (code/title/message/action) to JSON; frontend ApiError class already surfaces these via body field. TypeScript compiles clean.

internal/integrations/github/auth.go: AppCredentials.LoadAppCredentials (PKCS1 + PKCS8 PEM), MintAppJWT (RS256, 10m max with 30s safety margin), AuthClient.InstallationToken (per-installation cache, refresh-slack window, mutex-collapsed stampede protection, InvalidateInstallation for 401 recovery). 6 tests cover PKCS1/PKCS8 load, bad-input rejection, JWT signature roundtrip + claims, installation-token fetch+cache+per-installation isolation, refresh after expiry, HTTP error surface, manual invalidation. go vet + race + test all clean. No new module deps; pure stdlib.

internal/plugins/github/{plugin.go, plugin_test.go}: tool-only Plugin with PluginID com.nomi.github, manifest declares github.read/github.write/network.outgoing/filesystem.write capabilities + api.github.com/raw.githubusercontent.com/codeload.github.com network allowlist; ConfigSchema for app_id/installation_id/account_login/repo_allowlist/polling_enabled. authClientFor caches AuthClient per (app_id, installation_id). privateKeyForConnection uses secrets.Resolve. 5 tests cover manifest shape (capabilities + allowlist + empty tools), Start/Stop/Status, configInt coercion (8 cases), authOverride wiring, end-to-end load-from-secrets + cache reuse, missing-credential rejection. Plugin registered in cmd/nomid/main.go alongside other system-tier plugins. go build clean; tests green.

Closes the two gaps in the github-02-tools spec that remained after github-03/04/05 shipped: github.pulls.create (POST /repos/{o}/{r}/pulls with title/head/base + optional body/draft, gated by assertRepoAllowed) and github.repos.search_code (GET /search/code with three scoping rails: explicit owner+repo pins via repo: qualifier and validates allowlist; pre-existing repo: in query is trusted; otherwise allowlist entries are auto-OR'd in to keep results scoped). 8 new tests pass under -race, plugin manifest test now pins the full 12-tool surface. Pre-existing lint issues in issues_test.go + extractIssueRefs untouched.

internal/integrations/github/client.go: thin authenticated HTTP wrapper around AuthClient; typed RateLimitError + HTTPError; auto-invalidates installation token on 401. internal/plugins/github/issues.go: 4 tools (list/get/create/comment) with shared resolveConnection + clientFor + assertRepoAllowed (case-insensitive allowlist) + trim helpers that project upstream JSON to agent-friendly shape; .list filters out PR shadows. issues_test.go: 7 tests cover PR-filtering, comment bundling, request-body verification, missing-body rejection, allowlist permit/deny, RateLimitError type-assertion. Tools surfaced via plugin.Tools() + manifest contributions; Tools() registers all into the runtime registry. Build + vet + tests green.

Closed as superseded — same pattern applied this session to github-02-tools, task-collaborative-planning, and task-template-marketplace-v1. The trigger surface this task targets (pr_opened, issue_commented, push, workflow_failed) is already delivered via outbound polling in github-06-poll-triggers (done): internal/plugins/github/triggers.go implements two trigger kinds (issues, review_requests) with first-poll-baseline semantics, registered through the system-tier GitHub plugin. The webhook-receiver path itself is genuinely deferred — Nomi is local-first software running on :8080 with no inbound public reachability story; sibling task github-06-poll-triggers explicitly noted "switching to webhooks happens once we have a tunneled receiver story." That tunneled-receiver story is foundational infrastructure (HMAC verification differs per provider, secret rotation, public-URL leak risk) and benefits Slack/Discord/Email/Calendar — not just GitHub. It will land via roady_review_spec as a fresh V1.3 spec entry "Tunneled Inbound Receiver", not smuggled under the GitHub umbrella.

internal/plugins/github/pulls.go: 4 tools (list/get/comment/review) on the same toolDef pattern as issues. .get bundles PR metadata + diff_url + checkrun list (anchored to head SHA in one extra round-trip) + extractIssueRefs() on the PR body. .review validates event ∈ {APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, COMMENT}, rejects empty COMMENT body so an agent producing '' doesn't accidentally approve. .comment uses the issues-comment endpoint (top-level PR comment). Check-runs failure is non-fatal: surfaced as check_runs_error on the pull field. 6 tests cover head/base flattening, check-run bundling, fault tolerance, event validation, empty-body rejection, APPROVE-without-body acceptance, extractIssueRefs across keyword variants.

internal/plugins/github/repos.go: file_read pulls via /repos/.../contents API with base64 decode + 1MiB cap + directory rejection; clone shells out to `git clone --depth 1 --quiet` with installation-token in URL credential helper, sandbox-resolves into workspace_root via tools.ResolveWithinRoot, refuses overwrite, scrubs token from any error message that bubbles up. 6 tests cover successful base64 roundtrip, directory rejection, size cap, missing-workspace-root rejection, refuse-overwrite, sandbox-escape rejection. Build + tests green.

internal/plugins/github/triggers.go: Plugin.Triggers() lists per-Connection trigger instances (skipping disabled connections + ones with polling_enabled=false). Two kinds — github.issue_opened, github.pr_review_requested — with a 60s polling cadence. First poll establishes baseline (no firings) so daemon restart doesn't replay old state. issue_opened deltas highest-issue-number per repo; pr_review_requested tracks PR# set with non-empty requested_reviewers. Refuses to Start without a non-empty allowlist (would otherwise scan whole installation every minute). Token-bearer URL substituted in clone path scrubs token from any error message bubbled to caller. Manifest declares both TriggerContributions. 5 tests cover allowlist parsing (7 cases), polling-enabled coercion (8 cases), first-poll baseline, subsequent-poll firing on deltas in correct order, empty-allowlist refusal, idempotent Stop. Build + tests green.

GitHub connections work through the existing dynamic plugins-manager dialog: manifest's Requirements.Credentials gains a github_app_private_key entry (kind, key=private_key_pem, label, description with PEM-block hint). polling_enabled flipped to type "boolean" so it renders as a toggle instead of free-text. App ID/installation_id/account_login descriptions extended with where-to-find pointers. Frontend AddConnectionDialog gains a multi-line textarea + monospace + blur-when-hidden branch for kind=github_app_private_key (PEMs are multi-line by nature; the password masking blurs but keeps the textarea size). PAT path intentionally not exposed — the spec calls for App-flavor attribution and our auth.go is App-only. Build + Go tests + tsc + eslint + vitest (36 tests) all clean.

templates/built-in.json: new "GitHub PR Reviewer" entry (id template-github-pr-reviewer) with capabilities github.read+github.write+network.outgoing, system prompt walking through the read-diff → categorize-by-severity (blocking/recommended/nits) → choose-verdict-with-rules → submit-via-pulls.review chain, and an explicit non-negotiables section preventing politeness-approvals + empty COMMENT bodies. Permission policy: github.read=allow, github.write=confirm (so the user approves before any post lands), network.outgoing constrained to api.github.com + raw.githubusercontent.com. Renamed to *github-pr-reviewer to avoid clash with existing local-files Code Reviewer. Bumped count assertions in templates/templates_test.go and internal/api/smoke_test.go from 6 → 7. Full Go suite + frontend lint/type-check green.

Moved internal/connectors/google/{oauth,oauth_test}.go to internal/integrations/google/; imports in cmd/nomid/main.go + internal/api/router.go repointed to the new location. The connectors/google/ directory is gone. Integration tests (5 test cases covering device flow, poll success + access_denied, refresh, revoke) still pass. Google OAuth is now architecturally shared across Gmail/Calendar plugins — future plugins (gmail-02, calendar-01) import from integrations/google.

internal/plugins/gmail/ — tool-only plugin mirroring the Calendar shape. Provider interface (provider.go) abstracts the backend; GoogleProvider (google.go) hits gmail.googleapis.com directly with raw REST + RFC 5322 multipart message construction (text/plain, multipart/alternative when HTML, multipart/mixed when attachments). Five tools: gmail.send (HTML, attachments, drafts, threading), gmail.search_threads (Gmail query syntax), gmail.read_thread (fetches bodies including text + html), gmail.label (add/remove ids in one call), gmail.archive (removes INBOX). Uses the shared google.OAuthManager so a single Google account works for both Calendar and Gmail. SetProviderOverride seam lets tests inject stubs without OAuth. NetworkAllowlist: gmail.googleapis.com + oauth2.googleapis.com. 18 unit tests covering manifest, all 5 tools, RFC822 build paths (plain, alternative, mixed), input validation (missing To, bad base64, missing message_id), binding rejection, disabled connection. Whole 30-package suite green.

internal/plugins/gmail/trigger.go — three trigger kinds (gmail.from_watch, gmail.label_watch, gmail.query_watch) backed by Gmail's history API for efficient delta polling. One Trigger per (connection, rule) so each rule polls independently. Provider interface gained GetMessage, LatestHistoryID, History methods; GoogleProvider implements them against gmail.googleapis.com (history endpoint with historyTypes=messageAdded,labelAdded). First poll establishes a historyId baseline (no events fire) so an existing mailbox doesn't retro-fire on connection up; subsequent polls fetch deltas. query_watch matches by re-running the user's query with rfc822msgid: appended for the candidate message. Trigger rules live in connection config under trigger_rules[]. Manifest TriggerContribution declares all three kinds with example rule shapes in descriptions. Stop is idempotent + safe before Start. 12 trigger tests covering manifest, materialization (one per rule, malformed rule rejection, disabled connection), pollOnce semantics for each kind (from-match, label-match, query-match), baseline-no-fire on first tick, cursor advance, transient-error preserves cursor, idempotent Start/Stop, poll interval override, rules config parsing edge cases. Whole 30-package suite green.

Migration 000013_channel_identities (up+down) with unique index on (plugin_id, connection_id, external_identifier) + ON DELETE CASCADE from connection. domain.ChannelIdentity + FirstContactPolicy enum (drop | reply_request_access | queue_approval) with IsValid validation. ChannelIdentityRepository exposes Create/Update/FindByExternal/ListByConnection/Delete/IsAllowed. IsAllowed encodes the canonical "is this sender on this connection permitted for this assistant" check (empty AllowedAssistants = any; disabled = deny). Telegram plugin threads senderID through the poll loop and enforces the allowlist before creating a Run; unknown senders trip the connection's first-contact policy (drop silently, reply with a stock "ask the owner" message, or seed a disabled identity row for the owner to review). Backward-compatible: connections with no allowlist entries behave as before (allow everyone). 4 identity tests cover Create/Find, per-assistant filtering, connection-delete cascade, and policy validation. Backend sweep 100% green.

internal/api/identities.go exposes per-(plugin, connection) identity CRUD nested under /plugins/:id/connections/:conn_id/identities (GET list, POST create, PATCH update by ident_id, DELETE). Wired into the plugin group in router.go. Full build + test sweep green across 13 packages.

app/src/types/api.ts: ChannelIdentity + FirstContactPolicy types. app/src/lib/api.ts: identitiesApi helper (list/create/update/delete). app/src/components/identity-allowlist.tsx: new IdentityAllowlist component — renders list with inline enable/disable + delete, amber warning card when allowlist is empty ("open to anyone"), and an AddIdentityForm for entering external ids. app/src/components/plugins-manager.tsx: per-channel connections in the Plugins tab gain a chevron toggle; expanding reveals IdentityAllowlist. Gated behind "plugin has a channel contribution" so tool-only plugins don't render the panel. Frontend tsc/lint/20-vitest/vite-build clean.

New plugins.OutboundMessage + Attachment + AttachmentKind types in internal/plugins/roles.go. Channel.Send signature changed from (ctx, externalID, string) to (ctx, externalID, OutboundMessage). SendText(ctx, ch, externalID, text) convenience helper wraps into OutboundMessage for text-only callers. Every channel plugin (Telegram, Email, Slack, Discord) updated to the new signature; behavior is preserved for v1 (text dispatched, attachments silently ignored with a _ = msg.Attachments sentinel plus doc comment pointing at the per-plugin follow-up task). Registry/plugin_test stubs + telegram_test updated for the new shape. Attachment carries Kind/Filename/ContentType/Data/URL/Caption + IsInline() helper. Non-breaking at the assistant-tool level: tool calls still receive strings; the contract change only affects code paths that build Channels and call Send directly. Full backend test sweep across 18 packages green.

Telegram Channel.Send routes per Attachment.Kind: sendPhoto / sendVoice / sendVideo / sendDocument (unknown kinds fall back to sendDocument). Inline byte attachments upload via multipart/form-data using mime/multipart; URL-only attachments take the JSON path so Telegram fetches the file itself. msg.Text becomes the caption on the first attachment; subsequent attachments use att.Caption. Empty-text + no-attachments is a no-op. Errors decoded via shared decodeTelegramError helper that surfaces the Bot API description field. 4 new tests verify image → sendPhoto+multipart routing, voice → sendVoice routing, URL → JSON path, and unknown-kind document fallback. Backend sweep across 18 packages green.

internal/plugins/slack/plugin.go: Channel.Send extended to handle msg.Attachments via UploadFileContext (one upload per attachment). InitialComment carries msg.Text on the first attachment, falling back to att.Caption for subsequent files. ThreadTimestamp threaded through so thread context is preserved across uploads. URL-only attachments are explicitly rejected with a helpful error since Slack's upload API requires bytes (unlike Telegram). Fallback filenames per AttachmentKind keep Slack's preview rendering correct. Backend sweep across 18 packages green.

internal/plugins/discord/plugin.go: Channel.Send falls through to ChannelMessageSendComplex with a populated Files slice when msg.Attachments is non-empty. msg.Text + concatenated per-attachment Captions become the message Content (Discord doesn't have a per-attachment caption concept). Inline-bytes-only — URL-only attachments are rejected with a clear error since Discord's API requires file uploads. Fallback filenames per kind for inline-preview rendering. Backend sweep across 18 packages green.

internal/plugins/email/transport/transport.go: new EmailAttachment type + SendEmailWithAttachments helper that constructs a multipart/mixed MIME body — text/plain body part followed by one base64-encoded part per attachment with proper Content-Type / Content-Disposition headers. wrapBase64 honours RFC 2045's 76-char line-length recommendation. No-attachment calls fast-path through SendEmail to keep the simple case simple. Email plugin's Channel.Send routes through SendEmailWithAttachments when msg.Attachments is non-empty; URL-only attachments rejected with a clear error. 4 transport tests cover line-wrap behavior, short-payload no-wrap, fast-path delegation, and from/username validation. Backend sweep across 18 packages green.

internal/plugins/media/piper.go: PiperBackend implements TTSBackend by shelling out to the `piper` CLI with text on stdin, writing WAV to a temp file, and reading the bytes back. NewPiperBackend probes for the binary on PATH + the voice-model directory (default ~/.nomi/models/piper/); returns (nil, nil) when either is absent so main() can boot without Piper installed and the plugin surfaces "no TTS backend configured" instead of crashing. Path-traversal guard on the voice id rejects "/", "\\", and "." so a malicious caller can't escape modelDir. 30s subprocess timeout via context.WithTimeout — Piper does ~5x realtime, so a multi-paragraph reply still fits. Returns audio/wav; OGG transcoding for Telegram voice messages stays a follow-up. Wired into main.go with soft-fail probe + log line when bound. 4 unit tests cover soft-nil contract on missing binary, path-traversal rejection, empty-text rejection, and stable Name() for telemetry. Backend sweep across 19 packages green.

internal/plugins/media/whisper.go: WhisperBackend probes for whisper-cli / whisper-cpp / whisper on PATH (in that order — whisper-cli is the homebrew name) and ggml-<modelSize>.bin under modelDir (default ~/.nomi/models/whisper/, default model "base.en"). Soft-nil contract matches Piper. Transcribe writes audio to temp WAV, shells out with --output-txt + --no-prints + optional -l language hint, reads the .txt sibling back. 5min subprocess budget covers the slowest realistic case (large model on a slow CPU). Returns transcript + echoed languageHint as detected_language. Format conversion (OGG/M4A → WAV) deferred — whisper.cpp ships with optional ffmpeg integration that handles common formats; explicit conversion lands as a follow-up if needed. Added github.com/tetratelabs/wazero (v1.11). New internal/plugins/wasmhost/ package: Loader (one wazero runtime per daemon, WASI Preview 1 host module pre-instantiated for TinyGo compatibility) + Module (per-plugin instance with cached export refs). ABI defined: alloc/dealloc for host-managed buffers in plugin memory; plugin_manifest returns packed (ptr<<32 | len) i64 with JSON manifest body; tool_execute takes (ptr, len) of JSON {name,input}, returns packed (ptr<<32 | len) of JSON {result,error}. Spike scope host imports: host_log + host_emit_event (both no-ops; lifecycle-04 wires the gated imports). Load fails fast when required exports are missing. examples/wasm-plugin-echo/main.go: TinyGo-compileable echo plugin (~120 lines) demonstrating alloc/dealloc with a live-buffer map, JSON-encoded manifest export, JSON-round-trip tool_execute. Compiled to internal/plugins/wasmhost/testdata/echo.wasm (415KB, committed) — tests load it via go:embed so CI doesn't need TinyGo. Critical fix found during the spike: TinyGo's WASI build auto-runs main as _start at instantiation, which exits-code-0 the module before any export is callable; wazero.NewModuleConfig().WithStartFunctions() (no args) overrides the default _start list and keeps the module alive. Documented in the Load comments. Makefile target `wasm-echo` rebuilds the .wasm; runs tinygo with -target=wasi -no-debug. 3 tests cover the full round-trip (manifest + tool_execute), missing-exports rejection, and the i64 packing convention. Backend sweep across 20 packages green.

Five capability-gated host imports added to the WASM ABI: host_http_request (gated by network.outgoing + manifest NetworkAllowlist ∩ policy allowed_hosts), host_filesystem_read/write (gated by filesystem.read/write + workspace_root constraint), host_command_exec (gated by command.exec + allowed_binaries constraint), host_secrets_get (gated by secrets.read; key namespace partitioned per plugin to plugins/<plugin_id>/<key>). New CallConfig type carries per-call security context (PluginID, AssistantID, ConnectionID, Capabilities from manifest, NetworkAllowlist from manifest, Policy, Engine, HTTPClient) threaded through context.Context via WithCallConfig. Generic gate() function applies four security layers in order: (1) capability declared in manifest, (2) assistant + policy + engine present (rejects system-context calls by default), (3) per-connection override OR matching policy rule, (4) Allow mode required (Confirm rejected pending lifecycle-07's approval bridge). gateNetwork adds host-allowlist intersection check; gateCommand adds allowed_binaries lookup. Wildcard host matcher with leading-dot anchor (*.slack.com matches files.slack.com but NOT slack.com.attacker.com). Echo plugin extended: declares network.outgoing + NetworkAllowlist in manifest, exposes echo.fetch tool that calls host_http_request via //go:wasmimport. Build constraint //go:build tinygo on the example so plain Go test discovery skips it. Three end-to-end tests with the rebuilt WASM module: allowed-host round-trips through httptest server (200 + body), policy deny returns "denied" in plugin-visible response, host-allowlist mismatch returns "not in plugin allowlist" with both manifest + policy diagnostics. Plus 11 unit tests covering each gate layer in isolation, the host wildcard matcher (including leading-dot anchor), constraint shape coercion, and the per-connection-override precedence promised by ADR 0001 §7. Backend sweep across 20 packages green.

executeStep now wraps the tool invocation in invokeWithRetry. Transient failures (i/o timeout, 429, 5xx, connection refused, rate limit markers) retry up to Runtime.maxRetries times with exponential backoff (100/200/400/800ms). Step.RetryCount increments on each retry; step.retrying event (new EventType) fires so the UI can show progress. Run-level rate limiter already gates per-run burst.
